evidence: Logan's insight: 'Why don't we make a 30-second look at the information we capture via Careerspan?' Shift from explaining to showing — visual demonstration over word-heavy explanations.
Positioning: Careerspan as a 'human data company'. Video demo should be 'human data oriented' — show the data collection, then the results.

Key differentiator: opted-in, first-party candidate data. Reference to Eightfold lawsuit — positioning against publicly scraped data risks. Hook: 'This moment in time is important' (regulatory pressure on third-party data).
Value prop crystallized: 'Oh shit, I want that information'. Demo will use actual (anonymized) scan results, not mockups. Core message: 'We are a great solution because we collect first-party data'.
Decision: 45-60 second demo video, not a deck. Logan: 'A deck is not as impactful as watching it in action'.
Two distinct video/message versions: 1. Sourcing/Matching/External Hiring — for recruiting teams 2. Internal Mobility — for enterprise HR/L&D

Reviewed an email from Springboard as a benchmark. Key takeaways: Direct, high-level value delivery works. Lead with assets and outcomes, not explanations. Workforce board leads create immediate interest.

David envisions a system where candidates can converse with an AI that: (1) accesses David's coaching principles/methodology, (2) analyzes the candidate's background, (3) analyzes target company LinkedIn profiles, (4) generates strategic outreach messages.

Decision: David will start with a simpler, achievable project (meeting processing system) rather than attempting the complex messaging system initially. 'The first step... is I would say not working towards this big of a challenge.'

Vrijen shared deepwiki.com, a tool that converts GitHub repositories into Wikipedia-style documentation. David: 'It explains much more clearly what all is possible in N5OS. Even I didn't realize the system architecture was this elaborate.'
David: 'I think we need to help Zo articulate that. I cannot explain what Zo is concisely compared to other AIs... beyond allows you to store multiple files and it's like its own instance.'

Ben is interested in working with founders and exploring AI, making him a good match for Zo. David was invited to Zo offsite by Ben Erez. 'It was very cool to get that text from Ben asking to join Zo for the offsite.'

David outlined the system requirements: David's background/methods, Candidate's background (LinkedIn, CareerSpan answers), Target's context (LinkedIn profile), Candidate's goal. The system would triangulate across these inputs using David's principles.
David: 'I got four paid students... Yeah, people were paying it like one of them paid retail at 17.95 and didn't even show up.' First cohort completed successfully, currently running second cohort.

Working Style: V tends toward complexity, Logan simplifies. Good tension: V's instinct to add (Gamma pages, mock-ups) vs. Logan's push to strip down. Productive disagreement: ended at 'fuck all of that, let's just do that'.
